sections.paragraph:
ζιζάνιον, ου, τό (ApcMos, ch. 16; Geopon.; Etym. Mag. p. 411, 47) prob. (Suda: ζιζάνιον· ἡ ἐν τῷ σίτῳ αἶρα) troublesome weed in grainfields, darnel, cheat (Geopon. 2, 43 τὸ ζιζάνιον, τὸ λεγόμενον αἶρα, φθείρει τὸν σῖτον. Concerning αἶρα [without ζ.] the same statement in Theophr., HP 8, 8, 3, CP 4, 4, 8); resembling wheat, in our lit. only pl. (Geopon. 10, 87, 1; 14, 1, 5) in Mt in the parable of the ‘weeds (tares) among the wheat’ (cp. also Psellus p. 268, 17 οἱ τὰ ζιζάνια ἐπισπείροντες) Mt 13:25ff, 29f, 36, 38, 40 (s. RLiechtenhan, Kirchenblatt 99, ’43, 146–49; 167–69). The word is supposedly Semitic: ILöw, Aramäische Pflanzennamen 1881, 133; HLewy, D. semit. Fremdwörter im Griech. 1895, 52. On the subj. s. LFonck, Streifzüge durch die bibl. Flora 1900, 129f; Sprenger, PJ 9, 1913, 89ff; HGuthe, ZDPV 41, 1918, 164f; ILöw, D. Flora d. Juden I 1928, 723–29; AMoldenke, Plants of the Bible, ’52; Zohary, Plants 161.—DELG. M-M.

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

  1. ch. = chapter(s)

Geopon Geopon , a collection of older material, X a.d.—List 5

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. pl. = plural
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

bibl. bibl. = biblical

PJ

PJ = Preussische Jahrbücher—List 6

Pj = Palästinajahrbuch—List 6

ZDPV ZDPV = Zeitschrift der Deutschen Palästina-Vereins—List 6

Zohary Zohary = MZ., various works—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 429
